name: Tetas de Viana — circular trailrunning from Viana de Mondejar
name_original: Tetas de Viana — circular trailrunning desde Viana de Mondejar
region_intro: Jogging routes around Trillo offer diverse and picturesque natural landscapes in the province of Guadalajara, Spain. The region is characterized by the confluence of the Tagus and Cifuentes rivers, featuring spectacular waterfalls like "El Chorrerón" and serene river walks. Dominating the skyline are the "Tetas de Viana," distinctive flat-topped hills that provide challenging routes and panoramic views. Trillo also serves as a gateway to the expansive Alto Tajo Natural Park, offering access to a wider network of natural trails. Best jogging routes around Trillo The most popular jogging route is Tetas de Viana — circular trailrunning from Viana de Mondejar, a 4.7 miles (7.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 5 minutes to complete. This difficult route leads to the distinctive Tetas de Viana hills, offering panoramic 360-degree views. Another top favourite among local runners is Running loop from La Dehesa, a difficult 11.8 miles (19.1 km) path. This route explores the varied terrain surrounding Trillo, including riverine areas and gentle ascents. Local runners also love the Running loop from Trillo, a 5.5 miles (8.8 km) trail leading through the local countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 2 minutes. Jogging around Trillo is defined by river valleys, distinctive hills like the Tetas de Viana, and access to the Alto Tajo Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to more strenuous hill climbs. The routes in Trillo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 70 runners have used komoot to explore Trillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Trillo?
A: There are over 10 dedicated running routes around Trillo, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels. These include paths along the rivers, through the local countryside, and up to distinctive hills.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Trillo's jogging trails?
A: Trillo's jogging trails offer a diverse range of terrain. You'll find serene paths along the Tagus and Cifuentes rivers, more challenging ascents to hills like the Tetas de Viana, and varied landscapes within the Alto Tajo Natural Park. Expect a mix of riverside flats, gentle ascents, and some more strenuous climbs.

Q: Are there easy, flat running routes in Trillo?
A: Yes, Trillo offers easy running options, particularly along the riverbanks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31507964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azud de Pareja – Dique de Pareja loop from Pareja&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.5 km route with minimal elevation gain, perfect for a relaxed jog. The 'Route of the Waterfalls' also provides a pleasant, easy walk along the Cifuentes River.

Q: Are there challenging trail running options near Trillo?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032416772/tetas-de-viana-circular-trailrunning-from-viana-de-mondejar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tetas de Viana — circular trailrunning from Viana de Mondejar&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 7.6 km route leading to the distinctive Tetas de Viana hills, offering significant elevation and panoramic views. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31514414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from La Dehesa&lt;/a&gt;, an 19.1 km difficult path exploring varied terrain.

Q: Can I find running routes with panoramic views in Trillo?
A: Yes, the region is known for its stunning vistas. The routes leading to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6689105" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tetas de Viana&lt;/a&gt;, a recognized Natural Monument, offer spectacular 360-degree panoramic views of the Alcarria region and the Upper Tagus Basin. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032416772/tetas-de-viana-circular-trailrunning-from-viana-de-mondejar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tetas de Viana — circular trailrunning from Viana de Mondejar&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice for these views.

Q: Are there jogging routes that pass by waterfalls in Trillo?
A: Yes, Trillo is famous for its abundant water features. The Cifuentes River creates a spectacular series of waterfalls, including the emblematic "El Chorrerón." A dedicated "Route of the Waterfalls" (Ruta de las Cascadas) guides visitors along both banks of the river, past various cascades. You can also see the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5902743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cifuentes River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5694276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trillo Bridge and Cifuentes Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Trillo?
A: The running routes in Trillo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the diverse and picturesque natural landscapes, the variety of terrain from river paths to challenging hills, and the stunning views, especially from the Tetas de Viana. The access to the Alto Tajo Natural Park is also a significant draw.

Q: Are there any historical or natural landmarks along the running paths?
A: Yes, Trillo's routes offer several points of interest. Besides the natural beauty of the Cifuentes River waterfalls and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6689105" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tetas de Viana&lt;/a&gt;, you might encounter sections of the historic "Camino de la Lana" (Wool Route), a variant of the Camino de Santiago. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5694276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trillo Bridge&lt;/a&gt; itself is also a notable landmark.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Trillo?
A: Many of the running routes around Trillo are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032416772/tetas-de-viana-circular-trailrunning-from-viana-de-mondejar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tetas de Viana — circular trailrunning from Viana de Mondejar&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31514414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from La Dehesa&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24542555" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Trillo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is Trillo a good destination for family-friendly jogging or outdoor activities?
A: Trillo offers options suitable for families. While some running routes are challenging, the easy river walks, such as the 'Route of the Waterfalls' along the Cifuentes River, are perfect for a family jog or stroll. Additionally, Trillo features a multi-adventure park with zip lines and hanging bridges over the Tagus River, providing other active outdoor pursuits for families.

Q: Are there dog-friendly running trails in Trillo?
A: Many of the natural trails and paths around Trillo are suitable for running with dogs, especially those in the open countryside and along the rivers. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, particularly in protected areas like the Alto Tajo Natural Park, and to be mindful of local regulations and other trail users.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Trillo?
A: Trillo's diverse landscapes can be enjoyed year-round. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for jogging, with lush greenery in spring and beautiful foliage in autumn. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late evening runs are advisable. Winter offers a quieter experience, though some higher elevation trails might be cooler.
tips: Las Tetas de Viana is a mountainous set in the shape of a "tooth". They are two limestone sandstones eroded by the Tagus and Solana rivers. Each of them has its name such as La Redonda (the only one accessible) and La Larga or Elongated.
